content.boundary: Multigrade pedagogy and outcome-parity findings are moderately well documented, but no Hungarian data track long-term viability of ad hoc local negotiation without systemic support.
content.change_lever: Hungarian law already provides an administratively ready alternative to closure: multigrade (összevont) classrooms combining up to three (exceptionally four) consecutive grades, used in an estimated 200-300 schools nationally.; OFI research concludes that rural small-school pupil outcomes are broadly comparable to larger village schools and only moderately below urban schools once family socioeconomic background is controlled for, undermining pure efficiency arguments for closure.
content.name: Status quo plus incremental adaptation
content.system_problem: Keep the existing municipal/state-maintained village school open as long as feasible, relying on multigrade classrooms, felmenő rendszer phase-out and ad hoc local negotiation without a national framework.
